mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals weakening labor market conditions, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals strong supply-side momentum, which boosts corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year window, or 60-day returns in co-expansionary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) suppress bond yields, creating a flight-to-quality effect that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects robust labor market conditions, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansionary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,300 thousand units signals weak demand for residential construction, which depresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong demand-side fundamentals, which support equity valuations and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year window, or 60-day returns in co-expansionary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 103.5 signals supply-side disruptions, which reduce corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
